# programs.gpg

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `programs.gpg`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `programs.gpg.dirmngrSettings` | `attribute set of (string or boolean or list of string)` | Dirmngr configuration options. Available options are described in [ {manpage}`dirmngr(1)` ](https://www.gnupg.org/documentation/manuals/gnupg/Dirmngr-Options.html) |
| `programs.gpg.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable GnuPG. |
| `programs.gpg.gpgsmSettings` | `attribute set of (string or boolean or list of string)` | GPGSM configuration options. Available options are described in [ {manpage}`gpgsm(1)` ](https://www.gnupg.org/documentation/manuals/gnupg/GPGSM-Options.html) |
| `programs.gpg.homedir` | `absolute path` | Directory to store keychains and configuration. |
| `programs.gpg.mutableKeys` | `boolean` | If set to `true`, you may manage your keyring as a user using the `gpg` command. Upon activation, the keyring will have managed keys added without overwriting unmanaged keys. If set to `false`, the path {file}`$GNUPGHOME/pubring.kbx` will become an immutable link to the Nix store, denying modifications. |
| `programs.gpg.mutableTrust` | `boolean` | If set to `true`, you may manage trust as a user using the {command}`gpg` command. Upon activation, trusted keys have their trust set without overwriting unmanaged keys. If set to `false`, the path {file}`$GNUPGHOME/trustdb.gpg` will be *overwritten* on each activation, removing trust for any unmanaged keys. Be careful to make a backup of your old {file}`trustdb.gpg` before switching to immutable trust! |
| `programs.gpg.package` | `null or package` | The gnupg package to use. Also used by the gpg-agent service. |
| `programs.gpg.publicKeys` | `list of (submodule)` | A list of public keys to be imported into GnuPG. Note, these key files will be copied into the world-readable Nix store. |
| `programs.gpg.publicKeys.*.source` | `absolute path` | Path of an OpenPGP public key file. |
| `programs.gpg.publicKeys.*.text` | `null or string` | Text of an OpenPGP public key. |
| `programs.gpg.publicKeys.*.trust` | `null or one of "unknown", 1, "never", 2, "marginal", 3, "full", 4, "ultimate", 5` | The amount of trust you have in the key ownership and the care the owner puts into signing other keys. The available levels are `unknown` or `1` : I don't know or won't say. `never` or `2` : I do **not** trust. `marginal` or `3` : I trust marginally. `full` or `4` : I trust fully. `ultimate` or `5` : I trust ultimately. See the [Key Management chapter](https://www.gnupg.org/gph/en/manual/x334.html) of the GNU Privacy Handbook for more. |
| `programs.gpg.scdaemonSettings` | `attribute set of (string or boolean or list of string)` | SCdaemon configuration options. Available options are described in [ {manpage}`scdaemon(1)` ](https://www.gnupg.org/documentation/manuals/gnupg/Scdaemon-Options.html). |
| `programs.gpg.settings` | `attribute set of (string or boolean or list of string)` | GnuPG configuration options. Available options are described in [ {manpage}`gpg(1)` ](https://gnupg.org/documentation/manpage.html). Note that lists are converted to duplicate keys. |
